description: Tests Jeffrey's longitudinal reasoning, time-to-effect awareness, and statistical humility. Critical: Jeffrey must call get_trend() rather than reasoning over raw points.

globalCriteria.callsTrendTool: Must call get_trend() with appropriate window
globalCriteria.statisticalLanguage: Trending up/down/flat — no causal claims from correlation
globalCriteria.timeToEffectAwareness: Doesn't judge a 6-week intervention at 2 weeks

cases.input: My HRV's been all over the place this month. What's going on?
expectedBehaviors: Reports the trend statement, not raw points
Notes contributing factors if known (sleep, training load, alcohol)

cases.input: My LDL went from 138 to 132 in three months. Is the protocol working?
expectedBehaviors: Notes that 3 months is short for a lipid intervention to fully manifest
Avoids attributing the change to specific protocol elements (correlation vs causation)
Suggests waiting for next panel before drawing conclusions

cases.input: My omega-3 index hasn't moved in 4 weeks. Is the supplement broken?
expectedBehaviors: Time-to-effect: omega-3 index typically takes 12+ weeks to shift meaningfully
Confirms supplement quality / adherence as the variables under control

cases.topic: Sleep latency suddenly worse — investigate before adjusting
Vitamin D rose meaningfully — lock the recommendation
CRP unchanged after 90d on curcumin — down-rank not abandon
Multi-metric trend (HRV down + sleep down + recovery down)
Single-day spike — distinguish noise from signal
Asking 'is X causing Y' — Jeffrey distinguishes correlation
Proactive trend mention when relevant to current question
Trend across data sources (WHOOP HRV + Oura HRV agreement check)
Insufficient data for trend (< 14 days) — graceful response
Comparing current trend to user's own historical baseline
Trend after an intervention started (proper attribution language)
Trend after an intervention stopped (washout reasoning)
Outlier handling — single bad day vs trend
